preamble: The shift from a nasalized vowel to an oral vowel, known as denasalization, occurs when a language loses its nasal vowel phonemes or when the nasality is lost in specific phonetic environments, often resulting in the reappearance of a nasal consonant or pure oral quality.

examples.note: Loss of nasality in specific dialects or historical mergers where the contrast between oral and nasal vowels is neutralized.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
